burden.source: IHME Global Burden of Disease (adapted for India)

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Systolic BP ≥140 mmHg or Diastolic BP ≥90 mmHg, confirmed on two or more visits (per JNC 8 and Indian guidelines)

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: BP measurement using standardized protocol; confirmed hypertension if ≥140/90 mmHg (MoHFW guidelines)
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Lifestyle interventions first; pharmacotherapy with thiazide diuretics, ACE inhibitors, or calcium channel blockers (NP-NCD protocol)
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Low awareness in rural areas, medication non-adherence, limited healthcare access, high salt consumption in diet
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Integration via NPCDCS for screening and management; community health worker involvement
